skill.name: ai-wrapper-product
skill.description: Expert in building products that wrap AI APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) into focused tools people will pay for. Not just 'ChatGPT but different' - products that solve specific problems with AI. Cov...
skill.summary: Expert in building AI-powered products with proper architecture, cost management, and user experience patterns
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 32 static findings are false positives. The scanner detected code block markers in markdown documentation (e.g., ```python) as shell commands, and incorrectly flagged text patterns as cryptographic algorithms. This is a documentation skill containing example code snippets for building AI wrapper products - no actual shell execution, cryptography, or system reconnaissance is present.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
high_findings.description: Static scanner incorrectly flagged text patterns as cryptographic algorithms. No cryptographic code exists in this skill - only documentation and example code for AI product building.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms these lines contain only descriptive text, not cryptographic code. Scanner pattern matching on documentation is incorrect.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: External Commands (Shell Backtick)
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected 25 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' but these are code block markers in markdown documentation (```python, ```javascript). No shell commands are being executed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual code review confirms all flagged lines contain example code in markdown code blocks, not actual shell commands. Pattern matching on documentation produces false positives.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged input validation and JSON parsing code as 'system reconnaissance'. These are legitimate product development patterns.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Flagged code is standard input validation (throw Error) and JSON parsing - standard practices for building AI products, not reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Learn to build products that wrap AI APIs into focused tools people will pay for. Master product architecture, cost management, and UX patterns for successful AI businesses.
actual_capabilities: Design AI product architecture with proper input validation and output parsing
Select appropriate AI models based on cost, speed, and quality requirements
Implement prompt engineering patterns for production AI applications
Build cost tracking and usage metering systems for AI products
Create reliable AI outputs with validation and fallback handling
Differentiate AI products from generic chat interfaces
limitations: Does not provide actual AI API keys or access credentials
Does not execute AI calls - provides guidance and patterns only
Does not include pre-built AI product templates or code repositories
Requires user to implement their own API integrations

use_cases.title: Startup Founder Building AI SaaS
use_cases.description: A founder creating an AI-powered SaaS product learns how to architect their application, manage API costs, and differentiate from competitors.

use_cases.title: Developer Creating AI Tools
use_cases.description: A developer building AI-powered tools for specific use cases learns best practices for prompt design, output validation, and cost optimization.

use_cases.title: Product Manager Planning AI Features
use_cases.description: A product manager planning AI features for an existing product learns about model selection, cost implications, and user experience considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Design AI Product Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design the architecture for an AI product that [describe product use case]. Include input validation, prompt template structure, API integration approach, and output handling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new AI product project

prompt_templates.title: Optimize AI Costs
prompt_templates.prompt: How can I reduce AI API costs for [describe use case] while maintaining quality? Consider model selection, token optimization, caching strategies, and usage limits.

prompt_templates.title: Improve AI Output Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: My AI product sometimes produces inconsistent outputs. Help me design prompt patterns and validation logic to ensure reliable, structured responses for [describe use case].

prompt_templates.title: Differentiate From ChatGPT
prompt_templates.prompt: How can I differentiate my AI product from generic chat interfaces like ChatGPT? What specific domain expertise, UX patterns, or integrations would add unique value?

output_examples.input: Help me design an AI email writer product
output_examples.output:
Key architecture components:

1. **Input Validation** - Validate email purpose, recipient, tone, and key points before sending to AI

2. **Prompt Template** - Use system prompt to define professional tone, then inject user variables

3. **Model Selection** - Start with Claude 3 Haiku for speed, upgrade to Sonnet for complex drafts

4. **Output Parsing** - Force JSON output with title, content, and suggestions fields

5. **Cost Tracking** - Log token usage per email to calculate cost per user

output_examples.input: How do I prevent AI hallucinations in my product?
output_examples.output:
Hallucination prevention strategies:

1. **Prompt with examples** - Include 2-3 examples of correct output in your prompt

2. **Output format specification** - Force JSON with specific required fields

3. **Validation layer** - Parse and validate AI output before returning to user

4. **Fallback handling** - If output is invalid, retry with enhanced prompt or use fallback model

5. **Post-processing** - Run output through fact-checking or consistency checks

best_practices: Start with cheaper models like Haiku for high-volume tasks, upgrade to Sonnet or GPT-4 for complex requests
Always validate and parse AI outputs before returning to users - never trust raw responses
Track costs per user from day one to ensure unit economics work before scaling
anti_patterns: Building a thin wrapper around ChatGPT with no differentiation - users will just use Chat directly
Ignoring API costs until scale hits - surprise bills can bankrupt an AI product
Shipping AI outputs without validation - hallucinations and formatting issues destroy trust

faq.question: What is an AI wrapper product?
faq.answer: An AI wrapper product is a tool or application that uses AI APIs (like OpenAI or Anthropic) as the engine to solve a specific problem. Unlike generic chatbots, wrapper products focus on particular use cases with tailored prompts, UX, and integrations.

faq.question: How do I choose the right AI model for my product?
faq.answer: Consider cost, speed, and quality. Haiku is cheapest and fastest for simple tasks. Sonnet offers good balance. GPT-4 is best for complex reasoning but costs more. Start with cheaper models and upgrade based on user needs.

faq.question: How can I reduce AI API costs?
faq.answer: Use cheaper models for simple tasks, limit output tokens, cache common queries, batch similar requests, and truncate input context. Track every API call to understand your cost per user.

faq.question: How do I handle AI hallucinations?
faq.answer: Include examples in prompts, force structured output formats, validate responses before returning to users, implement retry logic with enhanced prompts, and add post-processing checks.

faq.question: What makes an AI product different from ChatGPT?
faq.answer: Differentiate through domain expertise, specialized prompts, integrations with other tools, better UX for specific tasks, and post-processing outputs for consistency. Solve a specific problem better than a general chatbot can.

faq.question: Do I need to worry about AI rate limits?
faq.answer: Yes, especially at scale. Implement retry logic with exponential backoff, use fallback models when limits are hit, and consider multiple API providers for redundancy.
